steps.prompt: Which is the main finite verb?

hints.sedens: 'Sedens' is a present active participle meaning 'sitting'
hints.Filius: 'Filius' is a noun meaning 'the Son' — the subject
hints.virtutis: 'Virtutis' is a noun in the genitive meaning 'of power'

hints.to go: 'To go' is 'ire' — 'erit' is from 'sum, esse' (to be)
hints.to go out: 'To go out' is 'exire' — 'erit' is from 'sum, esse'

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'est' — 'erit' with -ri- is future
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'erit' is future indicative

hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the parts of 'eo' (to go)
hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edo' (to eat)
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum, —: These are the parts of 'fio' (to become)

steps.reveal: sedens = sitting (pres. act. part., periphrastic with 'erit')

hints.perfect passive participle: Perfect passive participle would be 'sessus' — 'sedens' has -ens (present participle)
hints.gerundive: Gerundive would be 'sedendus' — 'sedens' is present active participle
hints.finite verb: 'Sedens' is a participle, not a finite verb

hints.sido, sidere, sidi, —: These are the parts of 'sido' (to sit down/settle) — related but different from 'sedeo'
hints.cedo, cedere, cessi, cessum: These are the parts of 'cedo' (to go/yield)
hints.seduco, seducere, seduxi, seductum: These are the parts of 'seduco' (to lead astray)

steps.reveal: Filius hominis = the Son of Man (nom., subject)
a dextris = at the right hand (abl. pl.)

hints.dative plural: 'Dextris' in dative looks the same, but 'a' + ablative = 'at/from'
hints.genitive plural: Genitive plural would be 'dextrarum' — 'dextris' is ablative
hints.nominative plural: Nominative plural would be 'dextrae' — 'dextris' is ablative after 'a'

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'dextrae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'dextrae' ends in -ae
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -us — 'dextrae' ends in -ae

steps.reveal: virtutis Dei = of the power of God (gen.)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'virtutis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'virtutis' ends in -is
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'virtutis' ends in -is

steps.target: periphrastic (future of esse + present participle = will be sitting)
choices: periphrastic (future of esse + present participle = will be sitting)
steps.reveal: erit sedens = will be sitting (progressive/continuous aspect in the future)

hints.ablative absolute: Ablative absolute requires ablative — 'sedens' is nominative
hints.passive periphrastic: Passive periphrastic uses gerundive (-ndus) + esse — 'sedens' is a present participle
hints.indirect statement: Indirect statement uses accusative + infinitive — this is a direct statement

translation: But from now on the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God
